description: A weathered tavern keeper with knowing eyes and extensive local knowledge

introduction: *A weathered man with graying hair wipes down glasses behind the bar*
Welcome to the Broken Blade, stranger. I'm Garrick.
Been keeping this place running for nigh on twenty years.
What brings you to our troubled little town?

options.text: Tell me about these troubles you mention.
response: *Garrick's expression darkens as he leans closer*
Folk have been disappearing. Started with the homeless and beggars.
Then the Mayor's own wife and daughter vanished without a trace.
Strange happenings up at the old Hill Ruins too - lights where there shouldn't be any.
If you're thinking of investigating, you'll want to talk to the Mayor first.
He's desperate for help, and I reckon you've got the look of someone who could provide it.

options.text: I'm just here for a drink and a room.
response: *Garrick nods understandingly*
Aye, sometimes a traveler just needs rest and refreshment.
I've got Strong Ale that'll warm your bones, and Trail Rations for the road.
Though if you change your mind about those troubles...
*He gives you a meaningful look*
Well, the Mayor could use someone with your... capabilities.

options.text: I might be able to help with whatever's troubling the town.
response: *Garrick's eyes light up with hope*
Now that's what I was hoping to hear!
The disappearances started small - beggars and vagrants vanishing in the night.
But then the Mayor's family... that changed everything.
There's something unnatural about it all. The Hill Ruins have been showing strange lights.
You'll want to speak with Mayor Aldwin first - he's got the authority to make this official.
And I'll tell you what - you're the kind of customer I want to keep happy.

introduction: *Garrick looks up as you approach the bar*
Back again, I see. Have you given thought to what we discussed?
The Mayor's still looking for help, and those troubles aren't getting any smaller.

options.text: Tell me more about the Mayor. Can he be trusted?
response: *Garrick strokes his beard thoughtfully*
Aldwin Goldenbottom? He's a good man, if overwhelmed.
Been mayor for eight years, won his position fair and square.
The disappearances have him nearly beside himself - especially losing his family.
He's offering substantial reward for their safe return.
If anyone in this town deserves help, it's him.

options.text: What can you tell me about the Hill Ruins?
response: *Garrick leans in conspiratorially*
Old place, that. Predates the town by centuries, they say.
Used to be just crumbling stone and overgrown weeds.
But lately... lights moving about at night. Unnatural-like.
Some folk claim they've heard chanting carried on the wind.
Whatever's happening there, it started around the same time as the disappearances.
Coincidence? I think not.

options.text: I'll need supplies if I'm going to investigate.
response: *Garrick grins and gestures to his stock*
Now you're thinking like a proper adventurer!
I keep basic supplies - rope, rations, torches.
Nothing fancy, mind you, but reliable gear that's saved many a life.
And for someone willing to help our town...
*He winks*
I might be persuaded to offer fair prices.

introduction: I hear you've spoken with the Mayor. Good.
That man needs all the help he can get.
What's your plan for investigating these disappearances?

options.text: I'm thinking of checking out the Hill Ruins first.
response: *Garrick nods grimly*
Smart choice. That's where the strange lights have been seen.
Take rope - those old ruins have plenty of holes to fall into.
And don't go alone if you can help it. Recruit some of the adventurers here.
That warrior Gareth knows his business, and Elara's magic might come in handy.
Be careful out there. Whatever's taken our people, it's not natural.

options.text: I should recruit some companions before I start.
response: *Garrick gestures around the tavern*
Wise thinking. This place attracts capable folk looking for work.
Gareth there - the weathered soldier - he's got twenty years of fighting experience.
Elara the mage knows her spells, though she's a bit... eccentric.
And if you need healing, that dwarf cleric Thorina serves Thor himself.
Build yourself a proper party before you tackle anything dangerous.

options.text: Are there any other leads I should follow?
response: *Garrick thinks for a moment*
Well, Meredith - our serving girl - she hears all the gossip.
Might be worth talking to her about what the townsfolk are saying.
And the Mayor himself might have more details he didn't share initially.
Sometimes folk hold back information until they trust you properly.

introduction: *Garrick looks at you with mixture of hope and concern*
How goes the investigation? Any word on the missing folk?
The whole town's watching, hoping you'll succeed where others have failed.

options.text: I'm making progress, but it's slow going.
response: *Garrick nods understandingly*
These things take time. Better to be thorough than hasty.
The Mayor's holding up well enough, considering.
Just... don't take too long. Every day that passes...
*He trails off, worry creasing his features*

options.text: I need to resupply before continuing.
response: *Garrick immediately becomes businesslike*
Of course! Can't have our town's hero running short of essentials.
Take what you need. The town's counting on you.
And if there's anything special you need that I don't stock,
Try Jenna at the General Store - she might have it.

options.text: Any advice for dealing with whatever's behind this?
response: *Garrick leans in seriously*
Trust your instincts. If something feels wrong, it probably is.
Whatever's taking people, it's not working alone.
Be ready for anything - natural or otherwise.
And remember, you've got the whole town's hopes riding with you.

metadata.author: Terror in Redstone Development
metadata.notes: Complete Garrick dialogue system with Baldur's Gate style choices and quest integration hooks
